title: trihaloragis hexandra subsp. hexandra
intro: Trihaloragis hexandra subsp. hexandra, also known as Radix Trihaloragis, is a plant species in the family Menispermaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits of Radix Trihaloragis include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of Radix Trihaloragis typically involve drying and processing the roots or stems of the plant for use in teas, infusions, or as a supplement.
